text:
Once upon a time in the serene quiet town of Willow Creek, there was a little old lady known for her exceptional gardenias. Her nickname among the locals was Old Gardenlia, for she could rarely bear to part with a single bloom.

One crisp autumn morning, while picking blooms for her window sill, Old Gardenlia stumbled onto a hidden glade she'd never noticed before. In the thicket there stood an old oak tree, gnarled and wise, its branches festooned in silvery autumn leaves.

She sat there, admiring the peace, contemplating life. Suddenly, a gentle rustling sounded. Looking up, she saw a tiny deer peeking curiously through the foliage. It seemed to recognize her right away. It came over quietly, nibbling on leaves.

Old Gardenlia petted the deer affectionately. "Hello there," she said, surprised.

The deer nibbled shyly. Then, it stopped nibbling and looked up into her eyes. "Old Garden
